originalTitle: Nigerian president's rivals get major boost as political heavyweight joins fold
title: Nigerian president's rivals get major boost as political heavyweight joins fold
content: Rabiu Kwankwaso joins the ADC, a coalition poised to take on President Tinubu in the 2027 elections.

originalTitle: Three killed as unfinished building collapses on church service in Ghana
title: A Sad Day in Ghana: Let's Learn About Safe Buildings
content:
Hello, amazing kids of Africa! I'm your special kid-friendly news reporter, and I'm here to share some important news from our continent. Today, we're talking about something sad that happened in a beautiful country called Ghana. It's important to talk about it so we can all learn how to stay safe.

What Happened in Ghana?

Imagine a place where people go to pray and sing together. That's a church, right? Well, one day in Ghana, something very sad happened at a church. There was a big building right next to it, and this building was not finished. It was still being built. But guess what? It suddenly fell down! This made many people very sad. This unfinished building was part of a school complex. A school complex is like a big area with many school buildings. So, this building that fell was near a school, where children might learn and play. It was very important for it to be strong and safe.

Why Was the Building Not Safe?

Sometimes, people start to build something, but they don't finish it. This building in Ghana was like that. It had been unfinished for a long, long time. Imagine you start building a tower with your blocks, but you don't make the bottom strong enough. What happens? It might fall over! Buildings need to be super strong. They need special builders and engineers to make sure every part is safe. An unfinished building is like a half-made toy. It's not ready to be played with because it might break. So, using an unfinished building, or being too close to one, can be very risky.

Did You Know? Ghana is in Africa!

Did you know that Ghana is a wonderful country right here in Africa? It's on the western side, with beautiful beaches and warm, friendly people. It's known for its yummy jollof rice and lovely Kente cloth. Even though this sad thing happened, Ghana is a strong and happy country. People there love to help each other, just like in many parts of Africa.

People Helping Each Other

When the building fell, many people were there inside the church, praying. It was a scary moment. But guess what? As soon as it happened, brave people from the community rushed to help! They didn't just stand by; they ran to see if anyone needed help. Helpers are superheroes! They quickly worked to make sure everyone was safe. They are an example of how strong communities are in Africa. When one person is in trouble, everyone comes together to lend a hand. This is a very special thing about our continent.

Why Do Buildings Need to Be Super Strong?

Imagine your home or your school. These buildings need to be very, very strong, right? They protect us from the sun, the rain, and strong winds. They need to stand up for many, many years. To make a building strong, special people called architects draw plans, and engineers make sure the plans are safe and strong. They use strong materials like cement, iron bars, and sturdy bricks. They follow special rules to make sure everything is built correctly.

Did You Know? Buildings Have Rules!

Did you know that every city and town has special rules for building? These rules are like game rules, but for buildings! They tell builders how high a building can be, how strong its walls must be, and how deep its foundation needs to go. These rules are super important because they help keep everyone safe. If someone doesn't follow the rules, a building might not be strong enough, and sad things like what happened in Ghana can happen. That's why grown-ups always check that buildings are built properly.

Learning About Safety

From this sad news, we learn how important safety is. We learn that we should never play near buildings that are unfinished or look wobbly. If you ever see a building that looks unsafe, it's very important to tell a grown-up right away. Our grown-ups, like our parents and teachers, work hard to keep us safe. And people who build things have a very important job to make sure everything is built safely for everyone.

Let's Remember to Be Kind

When sad things happen, it's okay to feel a little sad. But it's also important to remember the good things, like how people helped each other. Let's send our kind thoughts to the people in Ghana. Let's always remember to be careful, look out for each other, and appreciate the people who work hard to keep us safe and build strong homes and schools for us all over Africa. You are all super smart kids, and by understanding these things, you help make our world a safer place! Keep learning and keep being kind.
summary: In Ghana, a part of Africa, a building that was not finished fell down, making many people sad, and showing us how important it is for all buildings to be strong and safe.
whyMatters: This matters because it teaches us that buildings must always be finished and strong so that everyone can be safe, especially children at school and people praying. It also shows us how communities help each other when sad things happen.

originalTitle: 'This is bigger than football' - DR Congo bid to end 52-year World Cup wait
title: 'This is bigger than football' - DR Congo bid to end 52-year World Cup wait
content: DR Congo face Jamaica in a World Cup play-off, hoping to reach the tournament for the first time since 1974, a campaign memorable for the wrong reasons.

originalTitle: Five Euro 2028 tickets 'cost same as World Cup parking space'
title: Five Euro 2028 tickets 'cost same as World Cup parking space'
content: Uefa is to expected to broadly freeze prices at Euro 2028 - meaning supporters would be able to buy five tickets for the cost of one parking space at a World Cup stadium this summer.
